{'success': True, 'file_path': 'workspace/rtx3090_power_budget_analysis.txt', 'content': "     1	================================================================================
     2	RTX 3090 POWER BUDGET ANALYSIS - 20A CIRCUIT
     3	================================================================================
     4	
     5	CIRCUIT SPECIFICATIONS
     6	----------------------
     7	Circuit Rating:        20A @ 120V (US Standard)
     8	NEC Continuous Load:     80% of breaker rating
     9	Maximum Continuous:      16A
    10	Maximum Power:           1920W (16A × 120V)
    11	
    12	================================================================================
    13	SYSTEM POWER OVERHEAD BREAKDOWN
    14	================================================================================
    15	
    16	Component                      Power Draw
    17	-------------------------------------------
    18	CPU (High-end workstation)       150W
    19	Motherboard/Chipset               50W
    20	Fans/Cooling (multiple fans)      50W
    21	-------------------------------------------
    22	RAW OVERHEAD TOTAL               250W
    23	
    24	PSU INEFFICIENCY (~10%):          28W
    25	  (250W ÷ 0.9 = 278W actual draw)
    26	
    27	TOTAL SYSTEM OVERHEAD:            278W
    28	
    29	================================================================================
    30	AVAILABLE POWER FOR GPUs
    31	================================================================================
    32	
    33	Total Circuit Budget:            1920W
    34	System Overhead (with PSU loss): -278W
    35	                                 ------
    36	AVAILABLE FOR GPUs:              1642W
    37	
    38	================================================================================
    39	RTX 3090 POWER LIMIT CALCULATIONS
    40	================================================================================
    41	
    42	Stock TDP: 350W
    43	
    44	Power Limit    Watts per GPU    Max GPUs    Total GPU Power    Total System
    45	-------------  ---------------  ----------  -----------------  ------------
    46	  60%          210W             7 GPUs      1470W              1748W ✓
    47	  65%          228W             7 GPUs      1596W              1874W ✓
    48	  70%          245W             6 GPUs      1470W              1748W ✓
    49	  75%          263W             6 GPUs      1578W              1856W ✓
    50	  Stock (100%) 350W             4 GPUs      1400W              1678W ✓
    51	
    52	DETAILED BREAKDOWN BY POWER LIMIT:
    53	----------------------------------
    54	
    55	AT 60% POWER LIMIT (210W per GPU):
    56	  - 7 GPUs × 210W = 1470W
    57	  - System overhead = 278W
    58	  - Total draw = 1748W (91% of capacity)
    59	  - Headroom = 172W
    60	
    61	AT 65% POWER LIMIT (228W per GPU):
    62	  - 7 GPUs × 228W = 1596W
    63	  - System overhead = 278W
    64	  - Total draw = 1874W (98% of capacity)
    65	  - Headroom = 46W ⚠️ (Marginal)
    66	
    67	AT 70% POWER LIMIT (245W per GPU):
    68	  - 6 GPUs × 245W = 1470W
    69	  - System overhead = 278W
    70	  - Total draw = 1748W (91% of capacity)
    71	  - Headroom = 172W
    72	
    73	AT 75% POWER LIMIT (263W per GPU):
    74	  - 6 GPUs × 263W = 1578W
    75	  - System overhead = 278W
    76	  - Total draw = 1856W (97% of capacity)
    77	  - Headroom = 64W ⚠️ (Marginal)
    78	
    79	AT STOCK POWER (350W per GPU):
    80	  - 4 GPUs × 350W = 1400W
    81	  - System overhead = 278W
    82	  - Total draw = 1678W (87% of capacity)
    83	  - Headroom = 242W
    84	
    85	================================================================================
    86	STABILITY RESEARCH SUMMARY
    87	================================================================================
    88	
    89	Based on community research (Reddit, forums, technical guides):
    90	
    91	- 60% (210W): Widely stable for most workloads, retains ~85-90% performance
    92	- 65% (228W): Commonly used for 24/7 operation (Folding@Home, mining)
    93	- 70% (245W): Recommended minimum for demanding workloads (RT gaming, AI training)
    94	- 75% (263W): Good balance of performance and efficiency
    95	
    96	Minimum reported stable: ~50% (175W) for light tasks
    97	Maximum efficiency point: ~60% (210W) for AI inference
    98	
    99	================================================================================
   100	FINAL RECOMMENDATION
   101	================================================================================
   102	
   103	★ OPTIMAL CONFIGURATION: 7 GPUs at 60% Power Limit ★
   104	
   105	Configuration:
   106	  - Number of GPUs: 7
   107	  - Power Limit: 60% (210W per GPU)
   108	  - Per-GPU Power: 210W
   109	  - Total GPU Power: 1470W
   110	  - System Overhead: 278W
   111	  - Total System Draw: 1748W
   112	  - Circuit Headroom: 172W (9% safety margin)
   113	
   114	Why this is recommended:
   115	  ✓ Maximum GPU count (7 cards)
   116	  ✓ Stable power limit (60% is widely reported as stable)
   117	  ✓ Safe headroom on circuit (172W buffer)
   118	  ✓ Retains ~85-90% of stock performance
   119	  ✓ Excellent efficiency (performance per watt)
   120	
   121	ALTERNATIVE: 6 GPUs at 70% Power Limit
   122	  - Use if stability issues at 60%
   123	  - Higher per-GPU performance (~91-95% of stock)
   124	  - Same total power draw (1748W)
   125	  - More headroom per GPU for transient spikes
   126	
   127	================================================================================
   128	IMPORTANT NOTES
   129	================================================================================
   130	
   131	1. TRANSIENT POWER SPIKES: GPUs can briefly spike above power limit.
   132	   The 172W headroom at 60% helps accommodate these spikes.
   133	
   134	2. PSU REQUIREMENTS: You'll need high-quality PSUs with sufficient wattage
   135	   and efficiency ratings (Platinum/Titanium recommended).
   136	
   137	3. COOLING: 7 RTX 3090s generate significant heat. Ensure adequate case
   138	   ventilation and ambient room temperature control.
   139	
   140	4. MONITORING: Use software like MSI Afterburner or nvidia-smi to set
   141	   and monitor power limits continuously.
   142	
   143	5. CIRCUIT DEDICATION: Ensure this 20A circuit is dedicated to the system
   144	   and not shared with other high-draw appliances.
   145	
   146	6. ACTUAL MEASUREMENT: Use a Kill-A-Watt meter or similar to verify actual
   147	   power draw under load, as PSU efficiency varies.
   148	
   149	================================================================================
   150	QUICK REFERENCE TABLE
   151	================================================================================
   152	
   153	Goal                    GPUs    Power Limit    Total Power    Performance
   154	--------------------    ----    -----------    -----------    -----------
   155	Maximum GPU Count       7       60%            1748W          ~85-90%
   156	Balanced Performance    6       70%            1748W          ~91-95%
   157	Stock Power             4       100%           1678W          100%
   158	
   159	================================================================================
   160	END OF ANALYSIS
   161	================================================================================", 'start': None, 'end': None, 'total_lines': 161, 'truncated': False, 'error': None}

     6	
     7	## 1. MOTHERBOARDS for 4+ GPUs

     9	### LGA 2011-3 (Xeon E5 v3/v4) - Budget Option
    10

   164	## 3. RAM for LLM Inference

   352	## 8. COOLING
   353	
   354	### High CFM Server Fans (120mm)
   355	
   356	| Model | Price | CFM | Noise | Notes |
   357	|-------|-------|-----|-------|-------|
   358	| **TKFAN 120mm 6000RPM** | $8-$15 | 285 CFM | 55 dBA | Mining standard |
   359	| **Delta PFB1212UHE** | $10-$20 | 252 CFM | 60 dBA | Industrial grade |
   360	| **NMB 4715KL-04W-B56** | $12-$18 | 165 CFM | 48 dBA | Balanced option |
   361	| **Sanyo Denki 9S1212P4M** | $15-$25 | 190 CFM | 50 dBA | High quality |
   362	
   363	### Quiet Performance Fans
   364	
   365	| Model | Price | CFM | Noise | Notes |
   366	|-------|-------|-----|-------|-------|
   367	| **Noctua NF-A12x25** | $25-$35 | 60 CFM | 22 dBA | Premium quiet |
   368	| **Noctua NF-F12** | $20-$30 | 55 CFM | 24 dBA | Static pressure |
   369	| **Arctic P12 PWM** | $8-$12 | 56 CFM | 22 dBA | Budget quiet |
   370	| **be quiet! Silent Wings 3** | $20-$30 | 73 CFM | 26 dBA | German quality |
   371	
   372	### Fan Configuration Recommendations
   373	
   374	| Setup | Fan Count | CFM Total | Cost |
   375	|-------|-----------|-----------|------|
   376	| **Budget (Loud)** | 8x 285 CFM | 2,280 CFM | $80-$120 |
   377	| **Balanced** | 12x 165 CFM | 1,980 CFM | $150-$200 |
   378	| **Quiet** | 16x 60 CFM | 960 CFM | $320-$480 |

   385	---
   386	
   387	## Sample Build Configurations
   388

   416	
   417	### Enterprise Build (~$3,500 + GPUs)
   418

   429	| **TOTAL** | | **~$3,530** |
   430	
   431	---
   432	
   433	## Key Considerations for RTX 3090 Inference
   434	
   435	### Power Requirements
   436	- Each RTX 3090: ~350W (can spike to 400W)
   437	- 4x RTX 3090: ~1400-1600W
   438	- System overhead: ~300-400W
   439	- **Total needed:** 1800-2000W
   440	- **With 20% headroom:** 2200W+ recommended
   441	
   442	### PCIe Lane Requirements
   443	- Training: x16 electrical preferred
   444	- Inference: x8 electrical sufficient
   445	- x4 may bottleneck large model loading
   446	- PLX switches help with lane expansion
   447	
   448	### Thermal Management
   449	- RTX 3090 FE: 83C throttle point
   450	- AIB cards: 80-85C typical
   451	- Need 100+ CFM per GPU minimum
   452	- Consider repadding/repasting used cards
   453	
   454	### Memory Requirements
   455	- 7B models: ~16GB RAM
   456	- 13B models: ~32GB RAM
   457	- 30B models: ~64GB RAM
   458	- 70B models: ~150GB RAM
   459	- **Rule of thumb:** 2-3x model size in system RAM
   460	
   461	### Software Considerations
   462	- Ubuntu Server LTS recommended
   463	- CUDA 11.8+ required
   464	- PyTorch with CUDA support
   465	- llama.cpp for CPU offloading
   466	- vLLM for optimized serving
   467	
   468	---
   469	
   470	## Where to Buy Used Components
   471	
   472	### eBay (Primary)
   473	- Best selection of server hardware
   474	- Check seller ratings (98%+)
   475	- Look for "tested working" listings
   476	- Make offers on Buy It Now items
   477	
   478	### r/homelabsales (Reddit)
   479	- Community-driven sales
   480	- Often better prices than eBay
   481	- Check seller history
   482	
   483	### Facebook Marketplace/Craigslist
   484	- Local pickup for heavy items (cases, PSUs)
   485	- Negotiate bundles
   486	- Test before buying
   487	
   488	### Enterprise Liquidators
   489	- ServerMonkey
   490	- eBay enterprise sellers
   491	- University surplus auctions
   492	
   493	### AliExpress (New Budget)
   494	- Mining frames
   495	- Risers
   496	- Server fans
   497	- Long shipping times
   498	
   499	---
   500	
   501	## Final Recommendations
   502	
   503	### Best Value Motherboard
   504	**Supermicro X10DRG-Q-CS045** ($239)
   505	- Proven GPU support
   506	- PLX PCIe switches
   507	- IPMI remote management
   508	- DDR4 support
   509	
   510	### Best Value CPU
   511	**Dual Xeon E5-2680 v4** ($30)
   512	- 28 cores total
   513	- 80 PCIe lanes
   514	- DDR4 support
   515	- Unbeatable price/performance
   516	
   517	### Best Value PSU
   518	**Dual HP 1200W + Breakouts** ($100)
   519	- 2400W total capacity
   520	- Enterprise reliability
   521	- Easy to replace
   522	- Proven in mining
   523	
   524	### Best Value RAM
   525	**256GB (8x32GB) DDR4 RDIMM** ($320)
   526	- Used server pulls
   527	- Samsung/Hynix/Micron
   528	- Test before installing
   529	- Plenty for most models
   530	
   531	---
   532	
   533	*Research compiled: 2024*
   534	*Prices are estimates based on eBay sold listings and may vary*
